# setDisplayDetails(_:)

**Instance Method**

Specifies whether the user can see the certificate details.

## Declaration

```swift
@MainActor func setDisplayDetails(_ display: Bool)
```

## Parameters

- **display**: Pass [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] to display the certificate details, or [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false] to hide them.

## Discussion

For behavioral compatibility with macOS 10.3, certificate details are displayed by default. To hide the details of a certificate, you must explicitly set the display value to [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

## Related Documentation

- **detailsDisplayed()**: Indicates if the view currently shows the certificate’s details.

## Customizing the Appearance and Behavior of the View

- **setDetailsDisclosed(_:)**: Sets whether the certificate details subview is disclosed.
- **setDisplayTrust(_:)**: Specifies whether the user can see the certificate’s trust settings.
- **setEditableTrust(_:)**: Specifies whether the user can edit the certificate’s trust settings.
- **setPolicies(_:)**: Specifies the policies to use when evaluating this certificate’s status.
- **setPoliciesDisclosed(_:)**: Specifies whether the trust policy settings subview is disclosed.
